advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a stale/contended index.lock regression test for delete.py and init.py write paths (or generalize test_compact_index_lock into a shared parametrized test across compact/delete/init).
evidence: Ticket 3b4e-11f0-13a1-4f80 AC: "A regression test per newly-covered path (or a shared parametrized one) asserting a stale lock is reclaimed and a contended one is ridden out."
Only test_compact_index_lock.py was added (tests/interfaces/lifecycle listing shows no delete/init index-lock test)
delete.py and init.py both switched to run_git_write (src/rebar/_commands/delete.py:163, src/rebar/_commands/init.py:80) but have no accompanying stale-lock regression test
advisory.finding: The ticket AC requires a regression test per newly-covered path (compact/delete/init) or a shared parametrized one, but only compact.py received an index.lock regression test; delete.py and init.py index.lock self-healing is left unverified.
scenarios: A future refactor reverts delete.py or init.py to plain run_git; nothing fails, silently reintroducing the hard index.lock failure for those paths.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm/comment that routing host-repo git ops (init's `_git(repo, ...)`) through run_git_write intentionally reclaims the host repo's index.lock, not the tracker's.
evidence: src/rebar/_commands/init.py:345 `cp = _git(repo, "worktree", "add", tracker, branch)` — first arg is the host repo, not the tracker
gitutil.run_git_write passes str(tracker) into _with_index_lock_retry → _reclaim_if_stale_index_lock, which calls _resolve_tracker_git_dir on that arg (src/rebar/_store/gitutil.py:147, 88-98)

ABSENCE rationale: `git worktree add` locks the worktrees dir, not index.lock, so the index.lock retry signature is unlikely to fire here — low practical impact, but the wrapper's 'tracker' contract is being fed a host-repo path.
advisory.finding: In init.py, run_git_write is used for index-mutating ops on the HOST repo (e.g. `_git(repo, "worktree", "add", ...)`), where the first positional arg passed to the wrapper's stale-lock reclamation is the host repo path, not the tracker; the reclaim helper resolves the host repo's git dir in that case, changing behavior slightly for host-repo git ops rather than the tickets worktree the ticket targets.
scenarios: An index-mutating host-repo op via _git(repo, ...) hits an index.lock error; the reclaim targets the host repo's git dir (arguably correct), but the wrapper was documented/named around the shared tickets worktree.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a regression test that a stale index.lock is reclaimed during a `delete` and the delete succeeds (mirroring test_compact_index_lock / test_txn_index_lock).
evidence: search_files for `run_git_write` under tests/ returns only the compact test docstring line (tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_compact_index_lock.py:5) — no delete-path index.lock test exists

ABSENCE: tests/interfaces/store/ contains test_event_append_index_lock.py and test_txn_index_lock.py but no delete index.lock coverage; the concrete failure path (stale lock -> CommandError exit 2 hard failure) is exactly what this edit fixes and would silently regress
advisory.finding: delete.py's write path now routes through run_git_write (index.lock self-heal) but has no regression test; a revert to plain run_git would go undetected.
scenarios: A stale/contended index.lock on the shared tickets worktree during a `delete` add/commit
advisory.suggested_fix: Add a test (e.g. tests/interfaces/store/test_delete_index_lock.py) that seeds a ticket, plants a provably-stale index.lock, runs the delete CLI, and asserts the delete succeeds (no CommandError) and the lock was reclaimed.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a regression test that init's git writes self-heal a stale index.lock (or justify why init cannot encounter a pre-existing lock).
evidence: ABSENCE: search_files for `run_git_write` under tests/ finds no init-path test; the store test dir lists test_e4_init.py / test_init_idempotent_scratch.py but neither exercises an index.lock during init's git writes
advisory.finding: init.py's _git now routes through run_git_write (index.lock self-heal) but has no regression test for the stale-lock path; reverting to plain run_git would go undetected.
scenarios: A stale/contended index.lock during init's git add/commit on the tracker worktree
advisory.suggested_fix: Add an init-path index.lock test mirroring the compact test, or document in the diff why init's write path cannot race an index.lock (fresh worktree) — if so, init.py may not need the change at all.

coaching.coaching: Add a regression test covering the delete.py stale index.lock write path.
